overview.title: Task Management REST API
overview.purpose: This project is a REST API for managing tasks, designed for users who need a simple way to keep track of their to-do items.

components.name: API Server
components.description: Handles HTTP requests for task management operations.
components.interface_summary: Clients interact with the API Server via HTTP using JSON payloads to perform CRUD operations on tasks.

api_reference.inputs: { "title": "string" } - The title of the to-do item.
api_reference.outputs: { "id": "integer", "title": "string" } - The id and title of the created to-do.

api_reference.description: Updates a to-do item by id.
api_reference.inputs: { "title": "string" } - The new title for the to-do item, "id": "integer" path parameter.
api_reference.outputs: { "id": "integer", "title": "string" } - The id and title of the updated to-do.

steps: Clone the repository from version control.

operations.health_check: Verify the server is running by sending a GET request to /todos and ensuring a 200 response.
operations.logging: Requests and errors are logged to the console with varying verbosity based on environment settings.

common_issues.cause: Title field is missing or invalid in the request body.
common_issues.fix: Ensure the request body includes a valid 'title' string.

common_issues.cause: The specified to-do item id does not exist.
common_issues.fix: Verify the id in the request path is correct and exists.

known_limitations: Real-time synchronization is out of scope.
documentation.summary: This documentation covers the REST API for managing to-do tasks, intended for developers and system operators. It includes API endpoints, setup instructions, and troubleshooting information.
This documentation covers the REST API for managing to-do tasks, intended for developers and system operators. It includes API endpoints, setup instructions, and troubleshooting information.

latency_ms.mock_signature_note: S302 mock documentation calls return in ~tens of ms; this real call is ~thousands of ms
